Mr. Kaplowitz confirmed that the case settled. Petition For Order Under Probate Code Sec. 850 Authorizing And Directing Return Of Personal Property To Estate
Off Calendar Aug-15-2022 Continued To Oct-10-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christine Breen, via Zoom, for Henrietta Carbone and Yvonne Silveira, Personal Representatives; Edward Koplowitz, via Zoom, for Timothy Schell, Respondent; Charles Wisch, via Zoom, for Mark Bulaw, interested Party; Evan Winet, via Zoom, for American Research Bureau, Assignee.

Ms. Breen reports that there has been no discovery and requests an evidentiary hearing. Ms. Breen advises that there have been meet and confer sessions and that the general consensus is to mediate but that there should be some discovery prior to doing so. The court notices the absence of Ms. McLaughlin and asks for the status of the jurisdiction issue regarding the party who resides in Canada. Mr. Koplowitz that service to his Canadian client is not proper and that issue remains unresolved. Mr. Koplowitz offers additional information regarding his position and advises that he believes that mediation is key, but that Ms. McLaughlin's participation is also important to this matter. Mr. Wisch points to Ms. McLaughlin's actions as listed in the 850 petition and echoes Mr. Koplowitz' statement regarding her participation being a key factor in resolving this matter. The court confirms its position and reiterates Ms. McLaughlin's position in this matter as being key. Ms. Breen, Mr. Koplowitz, and the court discuss the court's ruling on the Motion to Quash earlier this year and points to the Notice of Hearing she filed on Jun-23-2022. The court reviews the Notice of Hearing and determines that an additional motion to quash will be required.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Oct-10-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Jul-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz specially appeared by zoom for Timothy Schell (Respondent).
Christine Breen appeared by zoom for Henrietta Carbone and Yvonne Silveira (Petitioners).
Azita Razim appeared by zoom for American Research Bureau (Assignee).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the issue of whether or not the supplemental opposition and the supplemental declaration of Gary Coffey were timely filed and served, whether the declaration of Matt Ysinga was also timely filed, whether there was a defect in the method of service of the motion, whether or not service of summons was timely, etc. Further discussion was held regarding the issue of jurisdiction, reasonableness, the parties not having gone to mediation even there was an agreement to do so, the parties being allowed to conduct discovery now and having an inspection of the vehicles, etc. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirms its tentative ruling. The Motion to Quash Service of Summons is granted in part and denied in part (see tentative ruling for details). The Court's Research Attorney will draft a form of order. The court expressed disappointment in seeing that there was no mediation, encouraged the parties to meet and confer to point out whatever defects they think there are, and encouraged the parties to set aside their egos and take a shot at mediation.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.
